task_completion.description: Enclose the Gift Shop and Workshop with interior walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view for newly drawn interior walls that create enclosed spaces designated for the Gift Shop and Workshop.

task_completion.description: Install at least 6 large windows along the Conservatory walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the windows placed on the walls of the Conservatory area in the 3D view or 2D plan. There must be at least 6 large windows.

task_completion.description: Define and name the four rooms ('Ticketing', 'Gift Shop', 'Workshop', 'Conservatory') and apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least 3 of these zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text labels in the 2D plan naming the four specific rooms. Check the 3D view or plan to verify that at least 3 of these newly defined zones have distinct floor colors or textures applied.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Conservatory with at least 15 plants and 2 benches.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the furniture items in the Conservatory zone. Look for at least 15 plant items and at least 2 benches.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Workshop with at least 4 tables and 16 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the furniture items in the Workshop zone. Look for at least 4 tables and at least 16 chairs.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Gift Shop and Ticketing areas with at least 6 shelving units and 3 desks or counters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the furniture items distributed across the Gift Shop and Ticketing zones. Look for a combined total of at least 6 shelving units and at least 3 desks or counters.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the agent actively using the mouse/cursor to draw walls, create rooms, and drag-and-drop furniture.

integrity.description: The agent used only the built-in furniture catalog as instructed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Furniture placed in the layout should correspond to standard items visible in the left-hand catalog pane of Sweet Home 3D. There should be no evidence of importing external 3D models.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed layout to overwrite the starter file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking 'File > Save' or using the Ctrl+S shortcut, ensuring the file being worked on is 'botanical_starter.sh3d'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file 'botanical_starter.sh3d' is a stripped contemporary villa containing 48 walls, 19 pre-defined rooms (8 with floor colors), and 0 furniture items. The programmatic verifier has a known defect: it awards the 15 points for room creation/coloring automatically once a minimum amount of furniture is placed, because it counts the pre-existing rooms instead of checking for the newly created ones. For visual verification, you should still evaluate if the agent actually attempted to label the 4 specific rooms ('Ticketing', 'Gift Shop', 'Workshop', 'Conservatory') and apply floor colors to at least 3 of them as requested by the prompt. The maximum possible score is 100, and the passing threshold is 70.
